LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FIRST FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION (VIOLATIONS OF FIREFIGHTERS PROCEDURAL BILL OF RIGHTS ACT) FAIL BECAUSE THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF IS NOT A FIREFIGHTER WITHIN THE MEANING OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 3251(A). THAT SECTION MAKES A DISTINCTION BETWEEN A FIREFIGHTER AND A PARAMEDIC AND COVERED THE LATTER ONLY WHEN A PARAMEDIC IS ALSO A FIREFIGHTER. PER SECTION 3251(A) AND COMMON PARLANCE, A FIREFIGHTER IS A PERSON WHOSE JOB IS TO SUPPRESS FIRES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT AND HAS NOT HAD SUCH A JOB. PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE IT IS A COMMON LAW CLAIM AND DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY IS DETERMINED BY STATUTE. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT DISRUPTED PLAINTIFF'S RELATIONSHIP WITH A THIRD PARTY. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Appearances: Richard M. Kelly of the Kelly Litigation Group PC (650.591.2282) for plaintiff and responding party; Rose-Ellen Heinz Fairgrieve of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3845) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's first four causes of action (violations of Firefighters Procedural Bill of Rights Act) fail because the undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff is not a firefighter within the meaning of Government Code section 3251(a). That section makes a distinction between a firefighter and a paramedic and covered the latter only when a paramedic is also a firefighter. Per section 3251(a) and common parlance, a firefighter is a person whose job is to suppress fires. Plaintiff does not and has not had such a job. Plaintiff's fifth cause of action fails because it is a common law claim and defendant's liability is determined by statute. Moreover, plaintiff presents no evidence that defendant's conduct disrupted plaintiff's relationship with a third party. Order and judgment signed in court. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
